description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a domed top and a wide, flared base, commonly found in urban environments. The hydrant features three prominent hose connection outlets: one on each side and one at the front, all capped. The central body is cylindrical, with vertical ridges and a slightly tapered midsection, and the top is topped with a hexagonal operating nut. The surface is smooth and glossy, with a uniform bright red paint finish and subtle highlights indicating a metallic material. There are no visible chains attached to the caps, nor are there any manufacturer or model markings present. The hydrant is designed to be a fixed, robust emergency water source, typically installed adjacent to streets or sidewalks.
description: A bright red, glossy fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets and a domed top, featuring a sturdy cylindrical body and flared base.
description_view_0: The hydrant is seen from a slight angle, showing the domed top, front outlet, and one side outlet clearly.
description_view_1: A direct front view highlights the central outlet and both side outlets symmetrically, with the operating nut visible on top.
description_view_2: This side view displays the left outlet and the front outlet, with the cylindrical body and base in clear profile.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the back of the hydrant, with both side outlets visible and no outlets or features on the rear face.
